title: No-bake vanilla cheesecake
subtitle: Cream cheese filling on an oat and butter base, set in the fridge
excerpt: A cold-set cheesecake with no gelatine and no baking. The filling is cream cheese beaten with whipped double cream — the fat content of both holds the cake firm when chilled. The base is pressed oats and butter. Vanilla flavours the filling. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Cold-set cream cheese preparations are documented in British domestic cookbooks from the late nineteenth century. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): no-cook cream moulds set by chilling are documented as a standard preparation. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: cold cream-based preparations are documented in the chapter on entremets. The technique of beating cream cheese with whipped cream and chilling until set without gelatine is documented in British domestic baking manuals of the twentieth century, available through the Internet Archive.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the day before serving. Keeps refrigerated for 3 days.

glossaryTerms.definition: A cheesecake set entirely by refrigeration rather than baking. The filling relies on the fat in cream cheese and whipped double cream to hold its shape when cold — no gelatine is needed if both are used in sufficient quantity and the cream is beaten to stiff peaks before folding in. The cheesecake must be fully cold before slicing: cutting a warm no-bake cheesecake produces a creamy puddle rather than a clean wedge.

content.text: No-bake cheesecake is the most forgiving thing in this category: no oven, no water bath, no cracking, no sinking. The only failure mode is pulling it from the fridge too soon. Allow four hours minimum; overnight is better.

Mix the {{rolled-oats}}, melted {{unsalted-butter}}, {{golden-syrup}}, and {{demerara-sugar}} together until the oats are evenly coated. Press firmly into the base of a 20 cm springform tin in an even layer. Refrigerate while you make the filling.
Make the filling and chill, at least 4 hours

Beat the {{cream-cheese}} with the {{icing-sugar}} and {{vanilla-extract}} until smooth. In a separate bowl, beat the {{double-cream}} to stiff peaks. Fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture until just combined. This is the
method: the filling holds its shape through fat and aeration, not gelatine. Spoon over the chilled base and smooth the top. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.
Run a palette knife around the inside of the tin before releasing the clip. Slice with a knife dipped in hot water and wiped clean between cuts. Top with seasonal fruit if you like.
